speaker: TITUS
text: Come, Marcus, come. Kinsmen, this is the way.—

speaker: TITUS
text: Sir boy, let me see your archery.

speaker: TITUS
text: Look you draw home enough and ’tis there straight.—

speaker: TITUS
text: Terras Astraea reliquit.

speaker: TITUS
text: Be you remembered, Marcus, she’s gone, she’s fled.—

speaker: TITUS
text: Sirs, take you to your tools. You, cousins, shall

speaker: TITUS
text: Go sound the ocean and cast your nets;

speaker: TITUS
text: Happily you may catch her in the sea;

speaker: TITUS
text: Yet there’s as little justice as at land.

speaker: TITUS
text: No; Publius and Sempronius, you must do it.

speaker: TITUS
text: ’Tis you must dig with mattock and with spade,

speaker: TITUS
text: And pierce the inmost center of the Earth.

speaker: TITUS
text: Then, when you come to Pluto’s region,

speaker: TITUS
text: I pray you, deliver him this petition.

speaker: TITUS
text: Tell him it is for justice and for aid,

speaker: TITUS
text: And that it comes from old Andronicus,

speaker: TITUS
text: Shaken with sorrows in ungrateful Rome.

speaker: TITUS
text: Ah, Rome! Well, well, I made thee miserable

speaker: TITUS
text: What time I threw the people’s suffrages

speaker: TITUS
text: On him that thus doth tyrannize o’er me.

speaker: TITUS
text: Go, get you gone, and pray be careful all,

speaker: TITUS
text: And leave you not a man-of-war unsearched.

speaker: TITUS
text: This wicked emperor may have shipped her hence,

speaker: TITUS
text: And, kinsmen, then we may go pipe for justice.

speaker: MARCUS
text: O Publius, is not this a heavy case

speaker: MARCUS
text: To see thy noble uncle thus distract?

speaker: PUBLIUS
text: Therefore, my lords, it highly us concerns

speaker: PUBLIUS
text: By day and night t’ attend him carefully,

speaker: PUBLIUS
text: And feed his humor kindly as we may,

speaker: PUBLIUS
text: Till time beget some careful remedy.

speaker: MARCUS
text: Kinsmen, his sorrows are past remedy

speaker: MARCUS
text: But

speaker: MARCUS
text: Join with the Goths, and with revengeful war

speaker: MARCUS
text: Take wreak on Rome for this ingratitude,

speaker: MARCUS
text: And vengeance on the traitor Saturnine.

speaker: TITUS
text: Publius, how now? How now, my masters?

speaker: TITUS
text: What, have you met with her?

speaker: PUBLIUS
text: No, my good lord, but Pluto sends you word,

speaker: PUBLIUS
text: If you will have Revenge from hell, you shall.

speaker: PUBLIUS
text: Marry, for Justice, she is so employed,

speaker: PUBLIUS
text: He thinks, with Jove in heaven, or somewhere else,

speaker: PUBLIUS
text: So that perforce you must needs stay a time.

speaker: TITUS
text: He doth me wrong to feed me with delays.

speaker: TITUS
text: I’ll dive into the burning lake below

speaker: TITUS
text: And pull her out of Acheron by the heels.

speaker: TITUS
text: Marcus, we are but shrubs, no cedars we,

speaker: TITUS
text: No big-boned men framed of the Cyclops’ size,

speaker: TITUS
text: But metal, Marcus, steel to the very back,

speaker: TITUS
text: Yet wrung with wrongs more than our backs can bear;

speaker: TITUS
text: And sith there’s no justice in Earth nor hell,

speaker: TITUS
text: We will solicit heaven and move the gods

speaker: TITUS
text: To send down Justice for to wreak our wrongs.

speaker: TITUS
text: Come, to this gear. You are a good archer, Marcus.

speaker: TITUS
text: “Ad Jovem,” that’s for you;—here, “Ad Apollinem”;—

speaker: TITUS
text: “Ad Martem,” that’s for myself;—

speaker: TITUS
text: Here, boy, “to Pallas”;—here, “to Mercury”;—

speaker: TITUS
text: “To Saturn,” Caius—not to Saturnine!

speaker: TITUS
text: You were as good to shoot against the wind.

speaker: TITUS
text: To it, boy!—Marcus, loose when I bid.

speaker: TITUS
text: Of my word, I have written to effect;

speaker: TITUS
text: There’s not a god left unsolicited.

speaker: MARCUS
text: Kinsmen, shoot all your shafts into the court.

speaker: MARCUS
text: We will afflict the Emperor in his pride.

speaker: TITUS
text:
Now, masters, draw.
                 
                (
                They
                 
                shoot
                .
                )
               O, well said, Lucius!

speaker: TITUS
text: Good boy, in Virgo’s lap! Give it Pallas.

speaker: MARCUS
text: My lord, I aim a mile beyond the moon.

speaker: MARCUS
text: Your letter is with Jupiter by this.

speaker: TITUS
text: Ha, ha! Publius, Publius, what hast thou done?

speaker: TITUS
text: See, see, thou hast shot off one of Taurus’ horns!

speaker: MARCUS
text: This was the sport, my lord; when Publius shot,

speaker: MARCUS
text: The Bull, being galled, gave Aries such a knock

speaker: MARCUS
text: That down fell both the Ram’s horns in the court,

speaker: MARCUS
text: And who should find them but the Empress’ villain?

speaker: MARCUS
text: She laughed and told the Moor he should not choose

speaker: MARCUS
text: But give them to his master for a present.

speaker: TITUS
text: Why, there it goes. God give his Lordship joy!

speaker: TITUS
text: News, news from heaven! Marcus, the post is come.—

speaker: TITUS
text: Sirrah, what tidings? Have you any letters?

speaker: TITUS
text: Shall I have Justice? What says Jupiter?

speaker:
COUNTRY
               
              FELLOW
text: Ho, the gibbet-maker? He says that

speaker:
COUNTRY
               
              FELLOW
text: he hath taken them down again, for the man must

speaker:
COUNTRY
               
              FELLOW
text: not be hanged till the next week.

speaker: TITUS
text: But what says Jupiter, I ask thee?

speaker:
COUNTRY
               
              FELLOW
text: Alas, sir, I know not Jubiter; I never

speaker:
COUNTRY
               
              FELLOW
text: drank with him in all my life.

speaker: TITUS
text: Why, villain, art not thou the carrier?

speaker:
COUNTRY
               
              FELLOW
text: Ay, of my pigeons, sir; nothing else.

speaker: TITUS
text: Why, didst thou not come from heaven?

speaker:
COUNTRY
               
              FELLOW
text: From heaven? Alas, sir, I never

speaker:
COUNTRY
               
              FELLOW
text: came there. God forbid I should be so bold to press

speaker:
COUNTRY
               
              FELLOW
text: to heaven in my young days. Why, I am going with

speaker:
COUNTRY
               
              FELLOW
text: my pigeons to the tribunal plebs, to take up a matter

speaker:
COUNTRY
               
              FELLOW
text: of brawl betwixt my uncle and one of the Emperal’s

speaker:
COUNTRY
               
              FELLOW
text: men.

speaker: MARCUS
text: Why, sir, that is as fit as can be to

speaker: MARCUS
text: serve for your oration; and let him deliver the pigeons

speaker: MARCUS
text: to the Emperor from you.

speaker: TITUS
text: Tell me, can you deliver an oration to the Emperor

speaker: TITUS
text: with a grace?

speaker:
COUNTRY
               
              FELLOW
text: Nay, truly, sir, I could never say

speaker:
COUNTRY
               
              FELLOW
text: grace in all my life.

speaker: TITUS
text: Sirrah, come hither. Make no more ado,

speaker: TITUS
text: But give your pigeons to the Emperor.

speaker: TITUS
text: By me thou shalt have justice at his hands.

speaker: TITUS
text: Hold, hold; meanwhile here’s money for thy

speaker: TITUS
text: charges.—Give me pen and ink.—Sirrah, can you

speaker: TITUS
text: with a grace deliver up a supplication?

speaker:
COUNTRY
               
              FELLOW
text: Ay, sir.

speaker: TITUS
text: Then here is a supplication for you, and when

speaker: TITUS
text: you come to him, at the first approach you must

speaker: TITUS
text: kneel, then kiss his foot, then deliver up your pigeons,

speaker: TITUS
text: and then look for your reward. I’ll be at

speaker: TITUS
text: hand, sir. See you do it bravely.

speaker:
COUNTRY
               
              FELLOW
text: I warrant you, sir. Let me alone.

speaker: TITUS
text: Sirrah, hast thou a knife? Come, let me see it.—

speaker: TITUS
text: Here, Marcus, fold it in the oration,

speaker: TITUS
text: For thou hast made it like an humble suppliant.—

speaker: TITUS
text: And when thou hast given it to the Emperor,

speaker: TITUS
text: Knock at my door and tell me what he says.

speaker:
COUNTRY
               
              FELLOW
text: God be with you, sir. I will.

speaker: TITUS
text: Come, Marcus, let us go.—Publius, follow me.
